title: Academic Discussion prompts (recalled 2026; OCR + DeepSeek structured)

items.professor_question: Do you think corporate transparency is essential, or should some information be kept confidential? Why?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think corporate transparency is essential. It builds trust with consumers and stakeholders and demonstrates a company's commitment to ethical practices. Transparency can lead to greater accountability and long-term success, as customers appreciate honest and open communication.

students.name: Paul
students.text: In my opinion, certain information should be confidential to protect competitive advantages. While transparency is important, businesses need to safeguard proprietary information and strategic plans to stay competitive. Complete transparency could potentially harm a company's position in the market.

items.professor_question: Which do you think has a greater impact on human development: nature or nurture? Why?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think nurture has a greater impact on human development. Our environment and experiences shape our beliefs, behaviors, and personalities. While genetics provide a foundation, it is our interactions and experiences that truly define who we become.

students.name: Paul
students.text: In my opinion, nature has a more significant role. Genetics determine many aspects of our personality, intelligence, and physical abilities. While the environment can influence these traits, our genetic makeup is the primary factor in shaping who we are.

items.professor_question: Do you think personalization is the key to building strong brand loyalty? Why or why not?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I believe personalization is essential for building brand loyalty. When customers feel that a brand understands their individual needs and preferences, they are more likely to stick with the brand. It creates a unique customer experience that sets the brand apart from competitors.

students.name: Andrew
students.text: I think personalization is just one piece of the puzzle when it comes to building brand loyalty. Other aspects like customer service, product quality, and pricing also play a significant role. A balanced approach is needed to maintain strong brand loyalty.

items.professor_question: What do you think is the most effective role of government in the economy?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think government intervention is important for ensuring economic stability and protecting consumers. Regulations can prevent businesses from becoming too powerful. Without government oversight the economy could become chaotic.

students.name: Andrew
students.text: In my opinion, too much government intervention can hinder economic growth and innovation. Businesses need the freedom to operate without excessive regulations, which can be costly and create inefficiencies. The economy would benefit more from government with a limited role.

items.professor_question: We've been examining the role of international organizations, such as the United Nations, in promoting global peace and security. Some argue that these organizations are essential for maintaining international order, while others believe they are ineffective and should be reformed. What is your opinion on the role of international organizations in global peace and security?

students.name: Andrew
students.text: They provide a platform for dialogue and cooperation between countries and can help mediate conflicts before they escalate.

students.name: Claire
students.text: While they have good intentions, they need significant reforms to be more effective in addressing global challenges.

items.professor_question: Do you think social media has a more positive or negative impact on society? Why?

students.name: Kelly
students.text: I believe social media has a positive impact on society. It keeps people connected with friends and family, share experiences, and access information quickly. Social media can also be a powerful tool for raising awareness about important issues.

students.name: Paul
students.text: In my opinion, social media has a negative impact on society. It can spread misinformation quickly and contribute to mental health problems like anxiety and depression. People often compare themselves to others on social media, which can lead to feelings of inadequacy.

items.professor_question: Do you think project-based learning is beneficial for students? Why or why not?

students.name: Paul
students.text: I believe project-based learning is highly beneficial for students. It encourages critical thinking, collaboration, and problem-solving skills. Students can apply what they learn in real-world scenarios, which helps them retain information better.

students.name: Claire
students.text: In my opinion, project-based learning might not work for every student. Some students prefer more structured teaching methods like lectures and exams. It's important to have a balance of different teaching approaches.

items.professor_question: What do you think is the most effective way to assess student learning? Why?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I believe project-based activities are the most effective way to measure student learning. They allow students to apply their knowledge in real-world contexts and demonstrate their skills through practical tasks.

students.name: Paul
students.text: In my opinion, traditional exams and standardized tests are still the best way to assess student performance. They provide a consistent and objective measure of what students have learned and allow for easy comparison across different groups of students.

items.professor_question: What do you think are the most significant ethical concerns related to AI development and use?

students.name: Andrew
students.text: I believe the most significant ethical concern about artificial intelligence is job displacement. As AI systems become capable of performing tasks traditionally performed by humans, millions of workers in manufacturing, customer service, transportation, and even professional fields like accounting may lose their livelihoods. This could lead to massive retraining programs that society may not be prepared to provide effectively.

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think privacy violations and data misuse represent the most serious ethical concerns related to artificial intelligence. AI systems collect vast amounts of personal information about our daily activities, purchasing habits, location data, and online behavior, often without clear consent or transparency about how this sensitive information will be stored, shared, or potentially used against individuals in the future.

items.professor_question: Do you think corporations should concentrate more on expansion or local business? Why?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think global expansion is vital for businesses. It unlocks opportunities, like broader talent pools and increased innovation. Most companies that I know seek to stay competitive in a connected world. While local focus has its strengths, going global builds resilience and relevance across cultures and economies. Why limit potential to one region when the world is within reach?

students.name: Paul
students.text: Focusing on local business practices supports regional economies and allows companies to tailor offerings to local needs. It builds trust, loyalty, and authenticity the family-owned ones downtown. Sometimes, deep roots in one place can create more lasting impact than a broad reach across many.

items.professor_question: Do you believe that managers should promote multitasking in the workplace? Why or why not?

students.name: Andrew
students.text: I think managers should promote multitasking. It helps employees handle routine tasks simultaneously and mirrors real-world demands. Even outside of the office, who has the luxury of focusing only on one task? With the right tools and training, multitasking can improve time management and adaptability - key traits in today's dynamic workplaces.

students.name: Kelly
students.text: I oppose multitasking in the workplace. It increases the chance of mistakes and leads to mental fatigue. For example, when employees answer emails during meetings, they often miss key details or misinterpret information. Deep, focused work is more effective for quality outcomes and long-term employee well-being.

items.professor_question: Do you think educators should prioritize active student participation or structured lecture-based instruction?

students.name: Paul
students.text: Educators should prioritize active participation because it helps students retain information more effectively and develop critical thinking skills. When students engage in discussions, solve problems, and participate in hands-on activities, they better understand how to use their knowledge in real situations. Active participation also keeps students focused and motivated throughout the learning process.

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think structured lecture-based instruction is more effective for building solid foundational knowledge that students need before attempting complex applications. Well-organized lectures allow teachers to present information systematically, ensure all students receive the same essential content, and cover curriculum requirements efficiently. Students can then apply this foundational knowledge through homework assignments and later practical exercises outside of class time.

items.professor_question: What is your perspective on the impact of cultural globalization on local traditions?

students.name: Kelly
students.text: I believe cultural globalization can lead to the loss of unique cultural identities. As global culture becomes more dominant, traditional practices and languages can be forgotten or replaced. It's important to preserve and celebrate local cultures to maintain diversity.

students.name: Andrew
students.text: In my opinion, cultural globalization promotes cultural exchange and understanding. It allows people to learn about and appreciate different cultures. This exchange can enrich local traditions and create a more interconnected and tolerant world.

items.professor_question: Are the effects of social norms mostly positive, or are they mostly negative? Explain your views.

students.name: Paul
students.text: I believe social norms help maintain order and predictability. They provide guidelines for acceptable behavior, which can make it easier for people to understand what is expected of them in various social situations. These expectations are helpful to guide interactions that are at times unclear.

students.name: Claire
students.text: I, personally, believe that social norms can sometimes restrict personal freedom as well as creativity. They often pressure individuals to conform to what the majority thinks is appropriate behavior. Breaking free from these norms can help people to express themselves more easily.

items.professor_question: Do you think emotional intelligence is more important than technical skills in the workplace? Why or why not?

students.name: I think
students.text: emotional intelligence is more important than technical skills in the workplace. Being able to communicate effectively, manage stress, and work well in teams can lead to better collaboration and a healthier work environment.

students.name: Paul
students.text: In my opinion, technical skills are more important in the workplace. Without the necessary technical expertise, employees wouldn't be able to perform their tasks efficiently. Emotional intelligence is valuable, but technical skills are essential for job performance.

items.professor_question: Reflect on a specific advertisement that influenced your behavior. Do you think its impact was positive or negative? Why?

students.name: Paul
students.text: I think the primary effect of advertising is to inform consumers. Ads provide valuable information about products and services, helping consumers make informed purchasing decisions and boosting economic growth.

students.name: Claire
students.text: I believe advertisements mainly manipulate consumer choices. They often create unrealistic expectations and encourage materialism, leading people to buy things they don't need and fostering a culture of overconsumption.

items.professor_question: These days, technology plays a critical role in many aspects of life, including security. Some argue that technology used in surveillance such as cameras and data collection software, is essential for maintaining safety and preventing fraud. Others believe it infringes on personal freedoms, as it may involve monitoring individuals without their consent. What do you think? Is the use of technology in surveillance for security purposes a necessary measure or a threat to personal freedoms? Why do you think so?
Reflect on a specific advertisement that influenced your behavior. Do you think its impact was positive or negative?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I believe advertisements mainly manipulate consumer choices. They often create a sense of urgency or desire for products that people don't need, leading to unnecessary purchases and fostering a culture of overconsumption.

students.name: Paul
students.text: Advertisements can inform consumers about new products and services, such as the latest tech products or new local businesses, potentially boosting sales and driving economic growth.

items.professor_question: What is your perspective on the impact of cultural globalization on local traditions?

students.name: Kelly
students.text: I believe cultural globalization can lead to the loss of unique cultural identities. As global culture becomes more dominant, traditional practices and languages can be forgotten or replaced. It's important to preserve and celebrate local cultures to maintain diversity.

students.name: Andrew
students.text: In my opinion, cultural globalization promotes cultural exchange and understanding. It allows people to learn about and appreciate different cultures. This exchange can enrich local traditions and create a more interconnected and tolerant world.

items.professor_question: What is your opinion on this?

students.name: Paul
students.text: Customer feedback is essential for product development. It helps companies understand customer needs and preferences, ensuring that products are tailored to the market. Feedback can guide improvements and enhance customer satisfaction.

students.name: Kelly
students.text: I believe that relying too heavily on customer feedback can stifle innovation. Companies should balance feedback with creative vision to avoid producing mediocre products. Innovation often requires taking risks and pursuing new ideas that customers may not initially recognize.

items.professor_question: What do you think is the most effective role of government in the economy?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think government intervention is important for ensuring economic stability and protecting consumers. Regulations can prevent businesses from engaging in harmful practices and help maintain fair competition. Without government oversight, the economy could become chaotic.

students.name: Andrew
students.text: In my opinion, too much government intervention can hinder economic growth and innovation. Businesses need the freedom to operate without excessive regulations, which can be costly and create inefficiencies. The economy would benefit more from government with a limited role.

items.professor_question: What is your perspective on the impact of cultural globalization on local traditions?

students.name: Kelly
students.text: I believe cultural globalization can lead to the loss of unique cultural identities. As global culture becomes more dominant, traditional practices and languages can be forgotten or replaced. It's important to preserve and celebrate local cultures to maintain diversity.

students.name: Andrew
students.text: In my opinion, cultural globalization promotes cultural exchange and understanding. It allows people to learn about and appreciate different cultures. This exchange can enrich local traditions and create a more interconnected and tolerant world.

items.professor_question: Do you think globalization has a positive or negative impact on local economies? Why?

students.name: Andrew
students.text: I believe globalization generally benefits local economies. It opens doors to new markets and can attract foreign investment, boosting infrastructure and job creation. Local businesses may not be able to open stores in other countries, but global networking can foster growth and competition within our community. The overall impact of globalization leads to a stronger, more dynamic local economy.

students.name: Kelly
students.text: I think that globalization tends to have a negative impact on local economies. It can hurt local businesses and lead to job losses as companies outsource production to countries where labor costs are lower. My parents own a store and have difficulty competing with global delivery services.

items.professor_question: Do you think globalization has a positive or negative impact on local economies?

students.name: Andrew
students.text: I believe globalization generally benefits local economies. It opens doors to new markets and can attract foreign investment, boosting infrastructure and job creation. Local businesses may not be able to open stores in other countries, but global networking can foster growth and competition within our community. The overall impact of globalization leads to a stronger, more dynamic local economy.

students.name: Kelly
students.text: I think that globalization tends to have a negative impact on local economies. It can hurt local businesses and lead to job losses as companies outsource production to countries where labor costs are lower. My parents own a store and have difficulty competing with global delivery services.

items.professor_question: Which do you believe is the main purpose of literature: to inspire change or to entertain?

students.name: Kelly
students.text: The main purpose of literature is to inspire social change. Many stories explore serious topics like historical conflicts, unfair treatment, or climate problems. When people read about these issues, they develop an understanding of other people's viewpoints and want to make improvements. Literature can open minds and help readers care about what others think and experience.

students.name: Andrew
students.text: Literature's primary goal is to entertain. It allows readers to enter new worlds, meet fictional characters, and enjoy surprising events. Even stories with serious themes often include exciting moments. Entertainment helps readers relax, imagine new possibilities, and appreciate creativity, which are also important benefits for individuals and society.

items.professor_question: How can people best manage a demanding job while still enjoying meaningful relationships and activities outside work? Is it possible to maintain a healthy social life while working full-time?

students.name: Andrew
students.text: Yes, but you have to set limits on work and prioritize your health and well-being. Focusing exclusively on work causes stress and burnout, which is bad for your career in the long run. Enjoying social activities is vital for your health. By setting the right boundaries, you can balance work with a healthy social life.

students.name: Claire
students.text: In my opinion, using technology to plan social activities can significantly enhance our social life. Tools like digital calendars and reminder apps help optimize free time, ensuring we don't miss out on valuable social interactions. This strategic planning can make maintaining a social life feasible, even with a full-time job.

items.professor_question: What do you think has a greater impact on shaping history: individual actions or broader social and economic forces? Why?

students.name: Andrew
students.text: I believe individuals and their actions have a greater impact on shaping history. Leaders and influential thinkers can inspire movements, create significant changes, and leave a lasting legacy. Their decisions and actions often alter the course of events in profound ways.

students.name: Kelly
students.text: In my opinion, broader social and economic events are more significant in shaping history. While individuals can influence events, it is the underlying conditions and trends that drive major changes. These forces affect large groups of people and create the context in which individuals operate.

items.professor_question: In your view, which of these two approaches best prepares students to face climate change and other environmental issues?

students.name: Claire
students.text: Focusing on social sciences like history and human geography helps us to see how our actions affect global communities. By learning about different cultures and the environment, we understand the need for collective action. This knowledge encourages us to work together to improve the environment on a global scale.

students.name: Andrew
students.text: Technical skills are essential in combating climate change. They provide tangible solutions to environmental issues. Education should prioritize teaching these skills, as they directly address and solve problems like renewable energy development and waste reduction. A practical approach like this can ensure a better future for all.

items.professor_question: Do you think its influence is mostly beneficial, or should we be more cautious about its reach and impact?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think social media has a negative impact on public opinion. It can often create echo chambers, where people only hear opinions that reinforce their existing beliefs. For example, if my friends on social media all seem to be saying the same thing, how can we be challenged to think more independently?

students.name: Andrew
students.text: I believe that the influence of social media is mostly beneficial. It allows people to access a wider range of viewpoints and engage in all kinds of discussions and across languages. This enhances understanding and awareness of different issues.

items.professor_question: Which view do you agree with? Why?

students.name: Andrew
students.text: I believe art has the power to drive social change. At many times in history, protest songs helped unite people to support important causes, while political cartoons exposed corruption and motivated voters to demand better leadership. Art doesn't just show problems; it gives people emotional reasons to care and practical ways to take action for justice.

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think art mainly reflects society by showing us what people experience and observe in their daily lives. Artists create paintings, songs, and stories based on their personal experiences and cultural backgrounds. While art can sometimes inspire people to think differently, its primary role is to document and preserve the current state of society for future generations.

items.professor_question: Do you think literature plays a significant role in shaping societal values? Why or Why not?

students.name: Andrew
students.text: Literature plays a significant role in shaping societal values because it gives people a unique opportunity to perceive and think about the feelings of others. Through storytelling, it can inspire empathy and provoke critical thinking about social issues. Books have historically influenced movements and brought awareness to important causes.

students.name: Claire
students.text: While literature has some impact, other forms of media like television and social media are more influential in shaping societal values today. These platforms reach a broader audience and can quickly spread ideas and trends. Most people prefer these newer forms of media because they are less challenging than literary works are.

items.professor_question: Do you think corporate transparency is essential, or should some information be kept confidential? Why?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think corporate transparency is essential. It builds trust with consumers and stakeholders and demonstrates a company's commitment to ethical practices. Transparency can lead to greater accountability and long-term success, as customers appreciate honest and open communication. In my opinion, certain information should be kept confidential to protect competitive advantages. While transparency is important, businesses need to safeguard proprietary information and strategic plans to stay competitive. Complete transparency could potentially harm a company's position in the market.

items.professor_question: Which do you think has a greater impact on human development: nature or nurture? Why?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think nurture has a greater impact on human development. Our environment and experiences shape our beliefs, behaviors, and personalities. While genetics provide a foundation, it is our interactions and experiences that truly define who we become.

students.name: Paul
students.text: In my opinion, nature has a more significant role. Genetics determine many aspects of our personality, intelligence, and physical abilities. While the environment can influence these traits, our genetic makeup is the primary factor in shaping who we are.

items.professor_question: Which view about using social media personalities for marketing do you agree with?

students.name: Claire
students.text: Social media influencers are highly effective for marketing because they create authentic connections with their audiences. When popular personalities share reviews and demonstrate products in their daily lives, followers trust these recommendations more than traditional advertisements. Influencers also reach specific target groups like young adults, helping companies connect with customers who are genuinely interested in their products.

students.name: Paul
students.text: I think relying on influencers for marketing can be risky and unpredictable for business success. Their popularity can change quickly due to scandals or changing trends, potentially damaging the brand's reputation. Additionally, many influencer campaigns fail to generate actual sales despite high costs, making it difficult for companies to measure whether their investment produced profitable results and increased revenue.

items.professor_question: What are your thoughts on this?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I believe active parental involvement is beneficial for children in general and student achievement in particular. For myself and for my friends, this has certainly been the case. When parents show interest in their child's education, it can motivate the child to perform better and feel supported.

students.name: Paul
students.text: Although I do see the benefits to parents being involved in their children's academic achievement, I think that too much parental involvement can be detrimental. It may prevent students from developing independence and problem-solving skills, which are crucial for their long-term success.

items.professor_question: Do you think emotional intelligence is more important than technical skills in the workplace? Why or why not?

students.name: Paul
students.text: I think emotional intelligence is more important than technical skills in the workplace. Being able to communicate effectively, manage stress, and work well in teams can lead to better collaboration and a healthier work environment.

students.name: Kelly
students.text: In my opinion, technical skills are more important in the workplace. Without the necessary technical expertise, employees wouldn't be able to perform their tasks efficiently. Emotional intelligence is valuable, but technical skills are essential for job performance.

items.professor_question: What is your perspective on the impact of cultural globalization on local traditions?

students.name: Kelly
students.text: I believe cultural globalization can lead to the loss of unique cultural identities. As global culture becomes more dominant, traditional practices and languages can be forgotten or replaced. It's important to preserve and celebrate local cultures to maintain diversity.

students.name: Andrew
students.text: In my opinion, cultural globalization promotes cultural exchange and understanding. It allows people to learn about and appreciate different cultures. This exchange can enrich local traditions and create a more interconnected and tolerant world.

students.text: Customer feedback is essential for product development. It helps companies understand customer needs and preferences, ensuring that products are tailored to the market. Feedback can guide improvements and enhance customer satisfaction.

students.name: Kelly

Local businesses may not be able to open stores in other countries, but global networking can foster growth and competition within our community. The overall impact of globalization leads to a stronger, more dynamic local economy.

students.name: Kelly
students.text: I think that globalization tends to have a negative impact on local economies. It can hurt local businesses and lead to job losses as companies outsource production to countries where labor costs are lower. My parents own a store and have difficulty competing with global delivery services.

items.professor_question: Which do you believe plays a larger role in a student's educational outcomes? Why?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think that the home environment and parental involvement are crucial. Students who have supportive parents and home life tend to perform better academically. Parents who are involved in their child's education can provide additional support and encouragement.

students.name: Paul
students.text: I believe that the quality of teaching and the resources at school are more important. Schools that provide a positive learning environment and the necessary tools for learning significantly impact a student's academic achievements.

items.professor_question: has technology had a positive or negative effect on modern education as a whole?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think technology enhances learning by making information more accessible and engaging. Tools like educational apps and online resources can cater to different learning styles and help students understand complex concepts.

items.professor_question: Do you think project-based learning is beneficial for students? Why or why not?

students.name: Paul
students.text: I believe project-based learning is highly beneficial for students. It encourages critical thinking, collaboration, and problem-solving skills. Students can apply what they learn in real-world scenarios, which helps them retain information better.

students.name: Claire
students.text: In my opinion, project-based learning might not work for every student. Some students may find it challenging to manage long-term projects and prefer traditional teaching methods like lectures and exams. It's important to have a balance of different teaching approaches.

items.professor_question: Do you think its influence is mostly beneficial, or should we be more cautious about its reach and impact?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think social media has a negative impact on public opinion. It can often create echo chambers, where people only hear opinions that reinforce their existing beliefs. For example, if my friends on social media all seem to be saying the same thing, how can we be challenged to think more independently?

students.name: Andrew
students.text: I believe that the influence of social media is mostly beneficial. It allows people to access a wider range of viewpoints and engage in all kinds of discussions and across languages. This enhances understanding and awareness of different issues.

items.professor_question: What is your opinion on the use of storytelling in communication?

students.name: Kelly
students.text: Storytelling is essential for successful communication during, for instance, presentations. It helps to connect with the audience on an emotional level, making the message more memorable and impactful. Stories can illustrate points more vividly than plain facts and keep the audience engaged from start to finish.

students.name: Paul
students.text: Factual, straightforward communication is more effective. Clear and concise information ensures that the audience understands the message without any ambiguity. While stories can be engaging, they may sometimes distract from the main points. Successful communication is determined more by its ability to convey a message than it is to entertain an audience.

items.professor_question: What do you think is the most effective role of government in the economy?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think government intervention is important for ensuring economic stability and protecting consumers. Regulations can prevent businesses from engaging in harmful practices and help maintain fair competition. Without government oversight, the economy could become chaotic.

students.name: Andrew
students.text: In my opinion, too much government intervention can hinder economic growth and innovation. Businesses need the freedom to operate without excessive regulations, which can be costly and create inefficiencies. The economy would benefit more from government with a limited role.

items.professor_question: Which do you believe plays a larger role in character development? Why?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think participating in sports is crucial for building character and discipline. I remember the time when I played volleyball in my first year of college, and it significantly helped me develop my social skills.

students.name: Paul
students.text: I believe that the values and lessons taught at home and in school provide a strong foundation for developing character and discipline. I practiced many sports, and while I did enjoy participating in these sports, I do not remember learning any life skills directly from them.

items.professor_question: What approach do you think leads to better long-term business success?

students.name: Claire
students.text: Companies should prioritize long-term growth through substantial investments in employee training and development programs, even when they reduce short-term profits temporarily. When businesses provide comprehensive skill development, professional certifications, and career advancement opportunities, they create more productive workforces, reduce expensive employee turnover, and build institutional knowledge that gives them competitive advantages over companies with less skilled workers in the marketplace.

students.name: Andrew
students.text: I think companies should prioritize keeping their product prices as low as possible by minimizing training expenses and other operational costs. When companies reduce spending on training programs, they can offer customers better prices than competitors, which generates higher sales volumes. Low prices help businesses gain new customers and keep existing customers from switching to other companies.

items.professor_question: Do you think globalization has a positive or negative impact on local economies? Why?

students.name: Andrew
students.text: I believe globalization generally benefits local economies. It opens doors to new markets and can attract foreign investment, boosting infrastructure and job creation. Local businesses may not be able to open stores in other countries, but global networking can foster growth and competition within our community. The overall impact of globalization leads to a stronger, more dynamic local economy.

students.name: Kelly
students.text: I think that globalization tends to have a negative impact on local economies. It can hurt local businesses and lead to job losses as companies outsource production to countries where labor costs are lower. My parents own a store and have difficulty competing with global delivery services.

items.professor_question: Which do you think has a greater impact on human development: nature or nurture? Why?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I think nurture has a greater impact on human development. Our environment and experiences shape our beliefs, behaviors, and personalities. While genetics provide a foundation, it is our interactions and experiences that truly define who we become.

In my opinion, nature has a more significant role. Genetics determine many aspects of our personality, intelligence, and physical abilities. While the environment can influence these traits, our genetic makeup is the primary factor in shaping who we are.

students.name: Kelly
students.text: I believe cultural globalization can lead to the loss of unique cultural identities. As global culture becomes more dominant, traditional practices and languages can be forgotten or replaced. It's important to preserve and celebrate local cultures to maintain diversity.

students.name: Andrew
students.text: In my opinion, cultural globalization promotes cultural exchange and understanding. It allows people to learn about and appreciate different cultures. This exchange can enrich local traditions and create a more interconnected and tolerant world.
